The Vault v0.5.1 upgrades the AI provider system to a primary + fallback architecture with per-provider model selections.

Highlights:
- Provider roles: set one provider (OpenRouter or LLM-Hub) as primary and optionally the other as fallback, directly from Settings. The legacy single-provider setting migrates automatically.
- Automatic failover: when the primary provider fails or is not configured, the task executor, enrichment pipeline, and Vault API backend retry on the fallback provider — using the fallback's own models, never the primary's model IDs.
- Per-provider model selections: OpenRouter and LLM-Hub each keep their own saved enrichment model and their own task routing table, so switching roles never loses a provider's chosen models.
- Reworked AI & Models settings: both provider cards are always visible with their own credentials, connection tests, and model dropdowns loaded live from each provider; the task routing editor gains an "Edit routing for" provider switch.
- Task results now record which provider executed them and whether the fallback provider was used.

This build packages the desktop app and the bundled `vault-memory` MCP sidecar runtime. After installing, open The Vault and use Settings -> Client setup to connect Codex, Claude Desktop, or Claude Code.
